step_by_step_thinking:
I agree with the original statement and answer choice. Apple jelly nodules are indeed characteristic of scrofula—a form of extrapulmonary tuberculosis affecting cervical lymph nodes—and their presence strongly suggests this diagnosis. This finding helps distinguish scrofula from other types of tuberculosis, including lupus vulgaris (which affects cutaneous tissue), Ghon complex (pulmonary primary TB), and other inflammatory processes.


Answer: A
